title: Crime and Security Incidents in Japan
summary: Arrest of Bobby Ologun, crash into a lawmaker's office, luxury plant fraud, bus accident, and testimony of a survivor of the Yamayuri attack.

title: New Products and Trends in Japanese Gastronomy
summary: Custom chocolates, limited-edition burgers and drinks are launched, bars and breweries expand, and a Starbucks boycott occurs in South Korea.

title: Agreement between the United States and Iran to Reduce Tensions
summary: Both countries are close to signing a memorandum that would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi Begins European Tour
summary: Takaichi visits the United Kingdom and will attend the G7 summit, with an emphasis on economic and energy security.

title: Japanese National Football Team Prepares for the World Cup
summary: The team completes its preparation, names Itakura as new captain, and coach Moriyasu shows confidence ahead of the match against the Netherlands.

title: Politics and Legislation in Japan: Reforms and Criticism
summary: Changes to the imperial house law, data protection, pensions are debated, and criticism of Prime Minister Takaichi for evading war issues.

title: World Cup: Other Teams in Competition
summary: The Netherlands aims for the title, Mexico celebrates a goal, Brazil and Morocco draw, while Qatar earns its first point.

title: News in Japanese Entertainment and Culture
summary: Interview with actor Katori Shingo, manga releases, and the band Mrs. wins the Japanese Grammy for the second consecutive year.

title: Commemoration of Natural Disasters in Japan
summary: The Iwate-Miyagi earthquake is remembered, the creator of the seismic alert sound is revealed, and a time capsule on floods is opened.

title: Donald Trump's Name Removed from Cultural Facilities
summary: By court order, the former president's name is removed from a cultural center and the Kennedy Center.

title: Japanese Government Studies Relaxing Transfer Rules in the Nuclear Sector
summary: It is considering easing the prohibition on moving personnel from the regulatory body to the nuclear promotion area.

title: Theft of 5 Million Yen under Car Purchase Scam in Osaka
summary: A man is beaten and robbed of his money during a meeting to buy a car.

title: Tokyo Metropolitan Police Opens Its Doors to the Public
summary: The first visitation program to the police academy begins to eliminate its intimidating image.

title: Technological and Scientific Advances in Japan
summary: Developments in hydrogen vehicles, export of subway trains, therapies with iPS cells, and debates on robotics without China.

title: Challenges in Japanese Education and Society
summary: Schools face problems of mites, poverty, exclusion, and promote inclusion through art and sports.

title: Other Sports in Japan: Baseball and Horse Racing
summary: Pitcher Yamamoto earns seventh win with the Dodgers, a baseball player overcomes a visual impairment, and foreigners seek treasures at the Oi racetrack.

title: Japan's International Relations with Various Countries
summary: Japan responds to Trump's publications, the war in Ukraine continues, Switzerland votes on population limit, and destroyers are exported to Indonesia.

title: New Mascots and Local Characters in Japan
summary: The cloud fairy and the Shirao keychain appear, while the character Pon-chan changes its name due to trademark registration issues.

title: Agricultural Problems: Raccoon Damage and Watermelon Seed Production
summary: Raccoons cause severe damage to watermelon crops, and Nara Prefecture leads the production of watermelon seeds in Japan.

title: Labor and Lifestyle Trends in Japanese Society
summary: Reports on job hunting, life advice, and the fear of being replaced by artificial intelligence.
